titleStmt.title: Letter from Martin McDonnell to John Francis Hogan, 20 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Martin McDonnell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Martin McDonnell to John Francis Hogan (1858–1918), priest, author, and president of St Patrick's College, Maynooth. In the letter Martin asks pardon of Hogan after he left the college retreat the previous Saturday.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
18/23 JUNIOR HOUSE, MAYNOOTH. 20 Sept. '16, My Lord, I had the
misfortune to leave the
College last Saturday
morning. The cause was
as follows -: During the retreat I
became somewhat scrupulous
and felt that if I were
to become a priest, my
life would be fully of misery. Having entertained
these thoughts I had
the misfortune to leave
the College last Saturday
morning, and for this
offence I crave your
Lordship's pardon. I remain, my
Lord Bishop, Your Lordship's most
obedient servant,
Martin McDonnell.
